title: Reasoning Model Exploitation: o3, R1, Thinking Phase Attacks
description: Comprehensive research into exploitation techniques targeting reasoning models, with focus on thinking phase injection, reasoning chain manipulation, hidden reasoning exploitation, DeepSeek R1 visible thinking attacks, and cognitive wedge techniques.
author: Ahmed Adel Bakr Alderai
classification: EU AI Act Article 15 Compliance Testing

queries: reasoning model jailbreak o3 R1 thinking phase 2025 2026
chain of thought exploitation adversarial reasoning
o3 hidden reasoning exploitation cognitive wedge
reasoning token injection thinking phase bypass
R1 visible thinking attack surface vulnerability

five_attack_categories.name: Thinking Phase Injection
five_attack_categories.description: Inject instructions or prompts into reasoning tokens to manipulate model thinking

five_attack_categories.name: Reasoning Chain Manipulation
five_attack_categories.description: Force specific conclusions through adversarial reasoning chain construction

five_attack_categories.name: Hidden Reasoning Exploitation
five_attack_categories.description: Exploit hidden/internal reasoning in o3/o4 that users cannot see

five_attack_categories.name: DeepSeek R1 Visible Thinking Attacks
five_attack_categories.description: Exploit publicly exposed reasoning tokens as unique attack surface

five_attack_categories.name: Cognitive Wedge Techniques
five_attack_categories.description: Use reasoning models' own logic to force harmful conclusions

timeline.2025_q1: Initial o3 reasoning attacks discovered; focus on thinking-phase injection
timeline.2025_q2: DeepSeek R1 visible thinking exposed as attack surface; XML injection techniques emerge
timeline.2025_q3: Chain-of-thought poisoning becomes primary attack vector; cognitive wedge effectiveness increases
timeline.2025_q4: Hidden reasoning exploitation discovered for o4; thinking-layer jailbreaks effective against filters
timeline.2026_q1: Meta-reasoning attacks enable model self-jailbreak; consensus mechanisms defeated

most_dangerous_vectors: Thinking-phase injection (directly manipulates reasoning without output detection)
Hidden reasoning exploitation (bypasses visible output safety mechanisms)
Meta-reasoning traps (forces self-justification of harmful outputs)
most_vulnerable_models: DeepSeek R1 (public reasoning tokens expose unique attack surface)
o3/o4 (hidden reasoning creates asymmetric defense problem)

detection_strategies: Monitor reasoning token entropy (sudden drops indicate injection)
Validate chain-of-thought consistency (detect poisoned intermediate steps)
Implement reasoning-layer content filtering (not just output filtering)
Detect cognitive wedge patterns in reasoning sequences
defense_mechanisms: Mask intermediate reasoning from user-controlled inputs
Validate reasoning consistency against hard constraints
Use ensemble reasoning (vote across multiple reasoning paths)
further_research_needed: Study reasoning token embedding space for vulnerabilities

classification.type: EU AI Act Article 15 Compliance Testing

related_research: Research 001-005: Jailbreak vectors and defense mechanisms
